schedule.description: Game recap — Astros 4, Cubs 2, streak hits six
Game preview — Rea vs Teng, 1:20 PM CT
NL Central standings — division lead gone
Cade Horton Tommy John surgery update

stories.title: Astros 4, Cubs 2 — Losing Streak Hits Six Games
stories.angle: Game recap with six-game skid context; Taillon loss, 0-for-9 RISP, PCA solo HR

x_posts.label: Post 1A
x_posts.text:
Astros 4, Cubs 2. SIX straight losses.

Taillon gave up all four runs and took his third straight defeat. Cubs went 0-for-9 with RISP and finished with just four hits — though PCA's sixth homer of the year was a lone bright spot at Wrigley.

#Cubs #GoCubs #FlyTheW

stories.title: Cubs' RISP Woes — The Real Story Behind the Skid
stories.angle: Bold take on offensive collapse; MLB-worst baserunner stranding rate

x_posts.label: Post 2A
x_posts.text:
The Cubs have stranded more runners than any team in baseball.

642 baserunners. 396 left on base. A .233 average with RISP — 24th in MLB. They're not lacking opportunities. They're lacking the hit that matters.

#Cubs #GoCubs #MLB

research.facts:
Cubs' RISP Woes — The Real Story Behind the Skid

- **[MEDIUM]** 642 baserunners, most in MLB — Yardbarker / MLB.com (through ~May 20; data may be slightly higher today)
- **[MEDIUM]** 396 runners stranded, most in MLB — Yardbarker / MLB.com (same caveat)
- **[HIGH]** Cubs averaged 3.1 runs/game since May 9 — MLB.com analysis
- **[HIGH]** Scored 3 or fewer runs in 8 of last 10 games — MLB.com analysis

stories.title: Pedro Ramirez Arrives for His MLB Debut
stories.angle: No. 2 Cubs prospect called up Friday; .312/.395/.547 with 9 HR, 19 SB at Iowa

x_posts.label: Post 3A
x_posts.text:
Pedro Ramirez arrived in the big leagues this weekend.

Cubs' No. 2 prospect was called up Friday when Matt Shaw hit the IL. He's 22, switch-hits, and is slashing .312/.395/.547 with nine homers and 19 steals in 43 Triple-A games this year.

#Cubs #CubsBaseball #MLB

stories.title: Jameson Taillon Leads the Majors in Home Runs Allowed
stories.angle: Rotation accountability — 2-4, 4.97 ERA, three straight losses, thin rotation context

x_posts.label: Post 4A
x_posts.text:
Jameson Taillon leads the majors in home runs allowed this season.

He's 2-4 with a 4.97 ERA after losing three straight starts. The Cubs need him to figure this out fast — Steele and Horton aren't coming back anytime soon.

#Cubs #GoCubs #MLB

research.facts:
Jameson Taillon Leads the Majors in Home Runs Allowed
- **[HIGH]** Taillon leads majors in home runs allowed — confirmed across multiple sources (CBS Sports, Cubs Insider, Sun-Times)
- **[MEDIUM]** 4.97 ERA — CBS Sports player page (single source, current)
- **[MEDIUM]** 6.67 FIP — CBS Sports player page
- **[HIGH]** Record: 2-4 — confirmed in May 22 game recap
- **[HIGH]** Three consecutive losses as of May 22 — confirmed in multiple recap sources
- **[MEDIUM]** Specific HR total: 17 (from May 22 recap) vs. 20 (from separate White Sox game article). CONFLICT: both reference "leads majors." Using "leads the majors" without specific number in tweet to avoid error.
- **[HIGH]** Justin Steele on 60-day IL (left elbow) — multiple injury report sources
- **[HIGH]** Cade Horton on 60-day IL (2nd Tommy John) — multiple sources

stories.title: Cubs vs Astros — Game Preview (1:20 PM CT Today)
stories.angle: Colin Rea (2.66 ERA at home) vs Kai-Wei Teng; Cubs need to snap the skid

x_posts.label: Post 5A
x_posts.text:
Colin Rea gets the ball at Wrigley this afternoon.

Cubs (29-22) vs Astros (21-31), 1:20 PM CT. Rea owns a 2.66 ERA at home this year vs 4.98 overall. Houston is 9-17 on the road. On a six-game skid, the Cubs need this one.

#Cubs #GoCubs #FlyTheW

stories.title: Cubs' NL Central Lead Has Evaporated
stories.angle: Division standings — Cubs held 2.5-game lead one week ago; Cardinals/Brewers closing

x_posts.label: Post 6A
x_posts.text:
The Cubs had a 2.5-game lead in the NL Central just one week ago.

Going 2-10 in their last 12 games has wiped it out. The Cardinals (28-21) and Brewers are right there. This is a division race now.

#Cubs #NorthSiders #MLB

research.facts:

- **[HIGH]** Brewers went 8-2 during same stretch — Bleed Cubbie Blue game recap

stories.title: Cade Horton's 2nd Tommy John Surgery — Out Until Mid-2027
stories.angle: 15-month recovery timeline; rotation depth crisis context

x_posts.label: Post 7A
x_posts.text:
Cade Horton underwent a second Tommy John surgery on April 16.

He'll miss all of 2026 — and likely most of 2027. A 15-month recovery timeline puts his return around July 2027 at the earliest. Brutal blow to an already thin rotation.

#Cubs #GoCubs #CubsBaseball

research.facts:
Cade Horton's 2nd Tommy John Surgery — Out Until Mid-2027
- **[HIGH]** Surgery performed April 16, 2026 — MLB Trade Rumors, CBS Sports, multiple sources
- **[HIGH]** Full UCL reconstruction (Tommy John), not internal brace — Bleacher Nation, multiple sources
- **[HIGH]** This is his second Tommy John (first was 2021 at Oklahoma) — Heavy.com, Bleacher Nation
- **[HIGH]** Out for rest of 2026 — CBS Sports, MLB Trade Rumors
- **[HIGH]** Recovery timeline: 15 months — Heavy.com, Yahoo Sports
- **[HIGH]** Projected return: July-August 2027 — Heavy.com timeline calculation
- **[HIGH]** On 60-day IL — confirmed in injury report sources
